info.description: This problem set focuses on usage and mechanisms of antibacterial agents. The questions are already reviewed.

q1.prompt: Which antibacterial agent inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis by binding to the D-Ala-D-Ala terminus of nascent peptidoglycan pentapeptides, thereby preventing their cross-linking and elongation?

q2.prompt: A 22-year-old college student presents with an insidious onset of a nonproductive cough and headache. A chest X-ray reveals patchy interstitial infiltrates. Which of the following macrolides is the drug of choice for this condition and works by binding to the 50S ribosomal subunit?

q3.prompt: Which antibiotic class is contraindicated in children and pregnant women due to its potential to cause damage to developing cartilage?

q4.prompt: Which drug class acts as a structural analog of p-aminobenzoic acid (PABA) to competitively inhibit the enzyme dihydropteroate synthase in the bacterial folic acid synthesis pathway?

q5.prompt: A patient requires antimicrobial prophylaxis before surgery to prevent Staphylococcus aureus wound infections. Which of the following first-generation cephalosporins is the standard choice for this indication?

q6.prompt: Which molecular modification of the terminal cell wall precursor is responsible for the resistance mechanism in Vancomycin-Resistant Enterococcus (VRE)?

options.label: Production of a low-affinity PBP2a protein
Synthesis of an altered arabinogalactan layer
Acetylation of the aminoglycoside binding site
Mutation of the alpha-subunit of DNA gyrase

q7.prompt: Which antibiotic class should be used with extreme caution because it can exacerbate neuromuscular blockade?

q8.prompt: How do suicide inhibitors, such as clavulanic acid and sulbactam, expand the antibacterial spectrum of certain penicillins?

options.label: By binding to and inactivating bacterial β-lactamases
By increasing the permeability of the bacterial outer membrane
By altering the target site on the 30S ribosomal subunit
By inhibiting renal tubule dehydropeptidase I
By providing a D-Ala-D-Ala structural analogue for PBPs

q9.prompt: A patient being treated for a resistant Gram-positive infection develops reversible myelosuppression and signs of serotonin syndrome. Which of the following agents is most likely responsible?

q10.prompt: A 27-year-old male presents with a painless genital chancre and is diagnosed with syphilis. Which of the following prototype β-lactam antibiotics is the intramuscular treatment of choice for this infection?
